text:
TEXARKANA, Texas (KSLA) - Texarkana, Texas native, Jarrion Lawson, is going to the Olympics in Paris as a member of the United States’ track team.

“I’ve always appreciated the love and support from my hometown, especially from my alma mater, Liberty Eylau,” Lawson said.

The former Liberty-Eylau standout qualified to compete in the long jump.

“The Olympic trials and the Olympic games is something we have been preparing for all year, and even some of last year,” Lawson explained.

“I was just thinking don’t scratch and let’s get this jump,” said Lawson’s parents, Jerrel and Carol.

Lawson’s parents were in the stands recently when their son made the team on his last jump. They say they’re his biggest fans.

“Yes, we were there. Row B, right where he jumped,” they said.

This will be Lawson’s second time participating in the Olympics. The Texarkana and University of Arkansas athlete failed to medal in 2016.

“He has persevered. He has kept going. He has been diligent, and you know, to see a culmination of all that hard work, you know, it’s just awesome,” his parents said.

Many trophies and awards were displayed inside the Lawson’s house; Lawson’s parents say they definitely have room for an Olympic medal. They say they will be in Paris to cheer their son on to victory.

“We want him to be able to look up and see us and know that we are behind him 150% and go ahead and do what you do,” Lawson’s parents said.

title: Texarkana native headed to the Olympics
description: Texarkana, Texas native, Jarrion Lawson, is going to the Olympics in Paris as a member of the United States’ track team.
